chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-2869 of 2023 ___________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge ___________________________________________________________________ FRESH CASE: 1. For order on CMA No.14030/2023 (Urgent). 2. For order on office objection Nos.19 & 27. 3. For order on CMA No.14031/2023 (Exemption). 4. For order on CMA No.14032/2023 (Stay). 5. For hearing of main case. ----------- Dated; 12th September 2023 Mr. Salman Ansari, Advocate for Petitioner. -*-*-*-*-*- 1. Urgency granted. 2. Learned counsel for the petitioner undertakes to comply with office objections before the next date of hearing. 3. Exemption granted subject to all just exceptions. 4&5. Through instant Constitutional Petition, the petitioner, who claims to be one of the descendants of Sangarwal, who according to petitioner, owned 90 acres of land alongwith his brothers Norowal and Eduwal before partition bearing Survey Nos.2223, 2324 and 2326, Tapu Gabo Pat, Hawks Bay near Jorak Goth, Karachi, however, after the death of the aforesaid persons, one Qadir Bux alongwith his brothers, who are relative of the petitioner, managed to get the entire land transferred in his name as well as in the names of his brothers depriving the petitioner and other legal heirs of their due share. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that subsequently Qadir Bux has transferred the entire land to one Muhammad Wana and after his death, entire land has been transferred to his son Ismail Wana. According to learned counsel, presently the petitioner is aggrieved by the official respondents, who were not providing the record of the

Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that subsequently Qadir Bux has transferred the entire land to one Muhammad Wana and after his death, entire land has been transferred to his son Ismail Wana. According to learned counsel, presently the petitioner is aggrieved by the official respondents, who were not providing the record of the [Page 2] subject land, therefore, requests that they may be directed to provide details of the land and transfer so that the petitioner may in a position to seek appropriate remedy before the competent Court of Law in accordance with law. Let pre-admission notices be issued to the respondents as well as to the Advocate-General Sindh, to be served through first three modes, for a date to be fixed after four weeks, when reply/comments, if any, shall be filed with advance copy to the learned counsel for petitioner. J U D G E J U D G E *Farhan/PS*
